# UC-11.5.6 · Video Conferencing License Utilization

## Description

Underused licenses waste budget; near-capacity entitlements risk meeting blocks during peaks—utilization guides true-up and consolidation across Zoom/Webex/Teams.

## Value

Underused licenses waste budget; near-capacity entitlements risk meeting blocks during peaks—utilization guides true-up and consolidation across Zoom/Webex/Teams.

## Implementation

Ingest license counts and active assignments from each vendor’s admin API on a daily schedule. Map SKUs to collaboration products. Alert above 90% utilization and report under-60% for reclamation. Normalize multi-platform duplicates where possible via email identity.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Connect for Zoom, Webex TA, Microsoft 365 licensing inputs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=zoom:account`, `sourcetype=webex:license`, `sourcetype=m365:license`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest license counts and active assignments from each vendor’s admin API on a daily schedule. Map SKUs to collaboration products. Alert above 90% utilization and report under-60% for reclamation. Normalize multi-platform duplicates where possible via email identity.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=saas (sourcetype="zoom:account" OR sourcetype="webex:license" OR sourcetype="m365:license")
| eval used_pct=round(assigned_licenses/nullif(total_licenses,0)*100,1)
| where used_pct > 90 OR used_pct < 60
| table platform, sku, assigned_licenses, total_licenses, used_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Video Conferencing License Utilization** — Underused licenses waste budget; near-capacity entitlements risk meeting blocks during peaks—utilization guides true-up and consolidation across Zoom/Webex/Teams.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=zoom:account`, `sourcetype=webex:license`, `sourcetype=m365:license`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Connect for Zoom, Webex TA, Microsoft 365 licensing inputs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: saas; **sourcetype**: zoom:account, webex:license, m365:license. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=saas, sourcetype="zoom:account". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **used_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where used_pct > 90 OR used_pct < 60` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Video Conferencing License Utilization**): table platform, sku, assigned_licenses, total_licenses, used_pct


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with each vendor’s admin portal (Zoom, Webex, Microsoft) for the same service or device and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (utilization % by platform), Table (SKU detail), Line chart (assigned licenses over time).

## SPL

```spl
index=saas (sourcetype="zoom:account" OR sourcetype="webex:license" OR sourcetype="m365:license")
| eval used_pct=round(assigned_licenses/nullif(total_licenses,0)*100,1)
| where used_pct > 90 OR used_pct < 60
| table platform, sku, assigned_licenses, total_licenses, used_pct
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (utilization % by platform), Table (SKU detail), Line chart (assigned licenses over time).
